title: Ahmad Jamal
html:
Ahmad Jamal

The American jazz pianist (1930–2023) whose name Yahir uses as the title-stub of one of the densest notes in the corpus: 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream. The title is itself an artifact of how Yahir's note-keeping works — he was listening to a Jamal stream when he opened a fresh Apple Note on October 25, 2022, and the note title persisted even though the content sprawled across jazz, crypto, ChatGPT product wedges (weeks after public launch), founder mantras (voluntary adversity, nonobvious, asking the right question), OSU coursework, Coinbase pitches, and a year of personal admin. The name Ahmad Jamal on a note about everything-but-Ahmad-Jamal is the chronic title/content mismatch Yahir has displayed since 2018 12 24 normal neutral to everything and 2019 11 13 coding zeal (titled "Coding Zeal", contains everything except coding).

Inside that note, Jamal is one of four named pillars of Yahir's late-2022 jazz listening cluster: Bill Evans, Louis Armstrong, Miles Davis, John Coltrane — a canon-pianist + canon-trumpeter + canon-pianist + canon-saxophonist quartet that suggests Yahir was building a foundational jazz vocabulary, not chasing contemporary picks. By 2024 the jazz interest is operational: in 2024 09 05 shanghai sidequests "Lincoln Jazz Center" makes the early-China chapter bucket list — meaning he was specifically targeting Wynton Marsalis's Jazz at Lincoln Center Shanghai venue while abroad. The jazz thread bridges Corvallis and Shanghai without breaking.

Jamal himself — known for his sparse, space-conscious, melodically pristine piano work, Poinciana the most iconic — slots cleanly into the "intellectual, restrained, build-by-subtraction" aesthetic Yahir consistently signals. The same instinct that draws him to "take existing things if equivalent value, but make them with 1/10 less resources and significantly cheaper" in the Ahmad-Jamal-stream note is the same instinct that listens to Ahmad Jamal in the first place. Jamal is what minimalism sounds like.

The fact that the densest note in the corpus — the bridge between college Yahir and founder Yahir, the one that contains the ChatGPT wedge brainstorming and the "asking the right question will be the next best skill to have" line — is named after a jazz pianist is itself the meta-point. Yahir's biggest creative leaps happen with the jazz canon playing in the background.
